topStories.title: Fed's Cook says she is prepared to raise rates if inflation doesn't ease

india.title: Why did Adin Ross refuse UFC Freedom 250 appearance despite Donald Trump planning historic White House fight night?
india.summary: Adin Ross said he will not attend UFC Freedom 250 at the White House after hearing rumors that Benjamin Netanyahu could be there. Ross shared the decision during a Kick livestream and said he prefers watching from home with his father. Meanwhile, UFC and White House staff are preparing the historic June 16 event, with Dana White and Donald Trump promising a massive crowd and celebration.

india.title: “This is so stupid”: Candace Owens challenges Blake Neff over Charlie Kirk and Erika Kirk TPUSA successor claims
india.summary: Candace Owens and Blake Neff clashed again on X after Neff appeared to mock Owens’ possible presidential ambitions. Owens responded by demanding proof that Charlie Kirk appointed Erika Kirk as his successor at TPUSA. Their argument quickly spread online, with supporters from both sides reacting strongly. The controversy also renewed public debate around Erika Kirk’s growing role within Turning Point USA.

GrannyGamingz aims to honor her daughter's memory through her streams. She also seeks legal assistance to share her daughter's story. Viewers are showing strong support for her cause.

business.summary: Timex Group India achieved a landmark financial year 2025-2026. Revenues surged 48% to Rs 800 crore, with profit before tax jumping 151% to Rs 107.4 crore. EBITDA also saw substantial growth. The company experienced strong performance across its brands, including Timex, Guess, and Versace. E-commerce channels recorded a 90% increase in sales.

business.title: Senco Gold Q4 Results: Profit skyrockets 151% to Rs 157 crore
business.summary: Senco Gold has announced a significant profit jump of 151 percent for the fourth quarter. This surge is attributed to soaring gold prices and robust sales. The company saw its revenue climb by 45 percent. Retail sales reached an all-time high, boosted by wedding demand and an active old gold exchange program.

business.title: Govt plans up to 2% LIC stake sale, may raise Rs 10,000 crore
business.summary: The government plans to sell up to a 2% stake in LIC, potentially raising Rs 10,000 crore. This move aims to deepen public shareholding amid improving profitability. The stake sale is likely in late June or early July. Merchant bankers were appointed last year. This divestment will help meet the government's asset monetisation target.
